description:
Fixes CLI UX friction points discovered during fresh-user experience testing. Three issues: (1) the 'opentabs start' subsequent-run hint tells users to run 'opentabs config show --show-secret' to see MCP client configuration, but that command only displays raw config fields — it does not print the formatted MCP client config snippets users need to copy-paste; (2) 'opentabs audit' when the server is offline prints an error but doesn't suggest the '--file' flag as an alternative for reading the persistent disk log; (3) 'opentabs config show' dumps the 'permissions' config key as a collapsed single-line JSON string, making it unreadable.

LOW severity issues documented but no stories created: (a) 'opentabs config show' permissions formatting is a cosmetic issue — users can use '--json | jq' for structured output.

userStories.title: Add formatted MCP client config output to 'opentabs config show --show-secret'
userStories.description: As a user who has already completed first-time setup, I want 'opentabs config show --show-secret' to display the formatted MCP client config snippets (Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, Windsurf) so that I can copy-paste them without restarting the server.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'opentabs config show --show-secret' displays the formatted MCP client config blocks (Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, Windsurf) with the correct URL and Authorization header, below the existing config fields
Running 'opentabs config show' (without --show-secret) does NOT display MCP client configs (the secret is masked so the configs would be non-functional)
The MCP client config output reuses the same printMcpClientConfigs function from start.ts to avoid duplication
The port used in the MCP URL is read from the config file (config.port field) with fallback to DEFAULT_PORT (9515)
The hint in start.ts line 269 ('Run opentabs config show --show-secret to see MCP client configuration') remains accurate after this change
userStories.notes:
The issue: start.ts:269 prints 'Run opentabs config show --show-secret to see MCP client configuration' but config.ts handleConfigShow only displays raw config fields + the secret. It does NOT print the formatted MCP client snippets that printMcpClientConfigs generates.

Fix approach:
1. Extract printMcpClientConfigs from platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts (around line 160) into a shared module (or export it from start.ts and import in config.ts)
2. In platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts handleConfigShow (around line 37), after the existing config display and secret display (line 104-107), add a section that calls printMcpClientConfigs when options.showSecret is true
3. The MCP URL should be constructed using the port from config (config.port as number, or DEFAULT_PORT from @opentabs-dev/shared)
4. The secret is already available in the displaySecret variable (line 53) — use the full secret (not masked) for the Authorization header

Key files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts (lines 37-109) — handleConfigShow
- platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts (lines 160-206) — printMcpClientConfigs to extract/export

Watch out for: knip may flag unused exports if printMcpClientConfigs was previously only used locally in start.ts.

userStories.title: Suggest '--file' flag when 'opentabs audit' fails due to offline server
userStories.description: As a user whose MCP server is not running, I want the 'opentabs audit' error message to suggest '--file' as an alternative so that I can read the persistent disk log without starting the server.
acceptanceCriteria: When 'opentabs audit' fails because the server is not running (ECONNREFUSED), the error output includes a hint about the '--file' flag, e.g. 'Use --file to read from the persistent disk log'
When 'opentabs audit' fails due to timeout, the same '--file' hint is shown
The hint is displayed as a dim secondary line (using pc.dim), consistent with existing hint patterns in the CLI
The existing error messages ('MCP server is not running' / 'Server not responding') are preserved unchanged — the hint is added as an additional line
userStories.notes:
The fix is in platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts, in the catch block around lines 287-303.

Currently the ECONNREFUSED handler (line 290-291) prints:
  'MCP server is not running.'
  Start hint

And the timeout handler (line 293-295) prints:
  'Server not responding (timed out). Is the server running?'
  Timeout detail

Add a third line after each existing error message, something like:
  console.error(pc.dim('Tip: Use --file to read audit history from the persistent disk log.'))

This matches the pattern used elsewhere in the CLI (e.g., status.ts provides actionable follow-up hints). Only add the hint to the ECONNREFUSED and timeout cases — the generic error case (line 298) doesn't need it since the server might be running but have a different issue.

Key file: platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts (lines 287-303)

userStories.title: Pretty-print 'permissions' config key in 'opentabs config show'
userStories.description: As a user viewing my OpenTabs config, I want the 'permissions' field to be displayed with readable formatting (like localPlugins and tools) so that I can understand my permission settings without using --json.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'opentabs config show' displays the permissions field with nested keys on separate lines, similar to how tools and browserToolPolicy are displayed
The trustedDomains and sensitiveDomains arrays are shown as bulleted lists (like localPlugins), with '(none)' when empty
The toolPolicy and domainToolPolicy objects are shown as key-value pairs (like tools/browserToolPolicy), with '(none)' when empty
userStories.notes:
In platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, the handleConfigShow function (around lines 63-101) has special formatting for 'localPlugins', 'tools', and 'browserToolPolicy' but falls through to a generic JSON.stringify for everything else (lines 95-100). The 'permissions' field is a nested object: { trustedDomains: string[], sensitiveDomains: string[], toolPolicy: Record<string, string>, domainToolPolicy: Record<string, Record<string, string>> }.

Add an 'else if' branch for 'permissions' (before the generic else at line 95) that formats it nicely:

  permissions
    trustedDomains
      - localhost
      - 127.0.0.1
    sensitiveDomains
      (none)
    toolPolicy
      (none)
    domainToolPolicy
      (none)

Follow the same visual patterns used for localPlugins (arrays as bulleted lists) and tools (objects as key-value pairs). The permissions type is defined in @opentabs-dev/shared — read the PermissionsConfig type to understand the shape.

Key file: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts (lines 63-101)
